hard.next_step: 50% Probability 4th May- reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI 16th Mar - Sent an intro mail and requesting for a revival meeting. 14th Jan Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan 30th Oct Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter 27th Oct Revised proposal- to meet Zee week of 12th Nov 14th Oct Zee indicates if we can work within a budget that he can get through exco, we may be able to close off in Q3- Carl to revise numbers and work with Zee 1:1 to optimize (likely reduce scope) 22nd Sept: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum . Budget creation and resource allocation are currently not feasible but he will look into it at earliest possible. They plan to reconnect in January 2026 to revisit the initiative. In the meantime, we will maintain a warm engagement , given the significant groundwork already completed, so we can seamlessly resume when the timing is right. The prospect is still interested in Zycus. 11th Sept: Carl has agreed to send revised commercials to Zee which we will prepare and send by next week. 8th Sept: Acen requested for revised proposal which includes recently discussed integration touch points which we will send it by EOD. 1st Sep Carl meeting Zee (CPO) 5th Sept to push for close- and remove blockers for Q2 close 26th Aug: Our team is preparing for 4th SAept meeting, no other update from Acen side. 18th Aug: Architecture Walkthrough meeting is scheduled on 4th Sept. 13th Aug: Jeff arranged quick clarification meeting for us to set the expectation for a meeting to discuss " Detailed walkthrough of the full system architecture, starting from supplier processes, through the Source-to-Pay (S2P) application layer, and down to the SAP ERP system ." 12th Aug: Since Jenn is on leave till 14th Aug, meeting is postponed to new date. Jeff will confirm new schedule soon. 8th Aug: Meeting scheduled for 13th Aug. 31st July: Requested for a clarification meeting with our presales. Awaiting for Acen's availability. 28th July: Acen has requested for a detailed walkthrough of the full system architecture, starting from supplier processes, through the Source-to-Pay (S2P) application layer, and down to the SAP ERP system. 16th July: In the meeting with Jenn, she concluded that she will get back to us after reviewing SAP scenarios and cost associated with it. 14th July: Jenn has set up a meeting to discuss SAP secondary cost on 15th July. Jeff is still chasing for ref call with SM. 30th June: Project has been delayed to Q1,2026.Onsite meeting with Acen team and President is scheduled for 9th July. 23rd June: Jeff and Jenn are negotiating with SAP for the secondary cost. meeting them in Manila on 25th June. 18th June: Acen is working on the user count and requested a session on 23rd to help them preparing exco presentations. We need to work on competitive analysis for Ariba. 9th June: We have a meeting today with CIP,CPO today to discuss revisions in the proposal and clarifications they have requested. 3rd June: Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba). Acen has requested for a isaas advance clarification session to plan internal resource planning and secondary cost from SAP Middleware. 2nd June: Acen responded with comments on our proposal and few additional queries related to commercials. Response expected by 6th June. Invoice page limit can be a blocker. 29th May The SAP blocker has caused some internal issues with final award timing- Carl to try and find a way to work around this slow down 26th May Arranging call for Carl and Zee to talk through how Zycus can better assist in TCO (if in fact SAP are playing this low integrity card of charging a fee to integrate Zycus) 22nd May: Had a call with President - Zee who shared a concerned over SAP secondary integration cost which is significant. He is yet to see the evaluation summary and commercials from Mehr. 19th May: Mehr mentioned that they havent finished compiling responses from all the vendors. 12th May: Jenn (CIO) and Mehr (CPO) are discussing the revisions they need in user count. We have another round of clarification on 14th May. 7th May: We had a first round proposal clarification. Acen is discussing internally the revisions they need in the proposal from our end.Expecting next clarification meeting in the week of 12th May. 5th May: Having a meeting with Zee this week, he is yet to confirm the time. RFP Committee has acknowledged the proposal and requested for a week to review it before any meeting or discussion. 28th April- Final proposal2 sending today- commercials and contract discussions planned for week of 5th May 5th April: Jeff extended the RFP response submission timeline to 30th April. Proposal is ready to be submitted from our side. 21st April: Progressing on RFP response with help of Carl,AKshay and Tanvi. To be submitted on 24th April. 14th April: Working on proposal preparation which is due on 24th April. POC helped us in getting better clarity of their requirements and expectations. 11th April: Successfully delivered the onsite POC in presence of Jenn (CIO) and Mehr(CPO). 8th April: Secured a face to face meeting for Carl and I to meet CPO Mehr and President along with CIO on 11th April. 7th April: Acen CIO Jenn had responded with selection criteria table as a response to my request. Weightage is 50% for functional fit and 50% for tech capabilities and ROI. Working with Carl on winning strategy. 4th April: Requested Shravan for a data migration partner which we need for this project. Working with presales on POC demo 31st Mar - Closed plan attached. 28th March - We have submitted the clarifications questions for RFP and POC preparation. 14th Mar- Acen has invited us for participating in S2P RFP. More details will be added in the notes. 10th Mar- Zee, Jonathan,Carl and Madhuri dinner meeting is set for 17th Mar 6th Mar- Zee requested our RFP template which he can ask his team to use without telling its coming from Zycus. 27th Feb: Carl, Zee and Madhuri spoke regarding fastening the vendor selection process, Zee, his boss (CFO - Jonnathan Back) are backing Zycus. Zee will speak to team to get the vendor selection done by April end. 21st Feb: Demo was interactive, CIO and CPO were clarifying functional and process related queries.Interest in ESG module as well. Lythouse deck and demo request has been sent. 18th Feb: we have scheduled a demo on 21st Feb for Acen CPO,CIO and key stakeholders to understand Zycus product better before they initiate RFI. 13th Feb strong F2F meeting with CPO (is Zycus champion) will move forward with project now- met with extended management team, finance, procurement, IT- strong position here following sister company close (CGash) 3rd Feb: Acen CPO Jenn is taking help in coaching her team regarding IMPL with HANA, phasewise impl approach, tender template etc. 24th Jan: Both Acen President and CPO have tentatively accepted Horizon invitation. To be confirmed on 5th Feb meeting with Carl and Madhuri. 21st Jan: As per Jenn, there is a slight delay in RFP roll out. She is pushing it for mid feb. 20th Jan: CPO meeting has been reschduled to 5th Feb since CPO also wanted to include other BU stakeholders. 13th Jan: Acen President and CPO has sent invite to Carl and I for a meeting on 22nd Jan for discussing GCASH Success and next steps. 16th Dec: Madhuri and Jeff had a call and he confirmed to release RFP in Jan'25. 4th Dec: Madhuri met Acen President, CPO, IT Head at their office. They are favouring Zycus as they have heard great things about Zycus from Delta air friends and from ABoitiz power. As part of the process, they will issue an RFP in Feb,25 6th Nov: IT and Procurement team from Acen is actively engaging with us. Detailed demo was done today and follow up clarification session is scheduled for 11th Nov. 25th OCt: To sign the NDA and get Pain-points, requirements and process information from them.
hard.ais_why:
• Per the 4 May 2026 rep note: 'reached out to Zee fr lunch and meeting with Globe about Agentic AI.' This is a very recent, brief signal that the rep is bridging ACEN with Globe Telecom for an Agentic AI conversation ‚Äî Zee (champion) is the ACEN-side touch and Globe Telecom is the AI-anchor reference.
• Per the 14 Jan rep note: 'Push Zee now for submission of our proposal in next exco meeting end Jan.' Per the 30 Oct 2024 note: 'Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter.' ACEN is a long-running multi-stage SAP-integration-heavy S2P deal with Zee as champion, vendor-selected stage reached but exco delays and SAP architecture walkthroughs dominate. Per 22 Sep 2024 from President Zee: 'due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.'
• Confidence is Medium given the extremely rich NextStep (8,099 chars over ~20 months). Product scope is iContract; iManage; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRisk; Lythouse ‚Äî no AI SKUs. The single AI keyword is the May 2026 reference to Globe Telecom's Agentic AI conversation as a future bridge. Score 4 (AI Curious, boundary DOWN) reflects the lack of AI products in scope, the SAP-integration focus, and the recent Agentic AI side-conversation that has not yet matured.

north_star_verdict.headline: Well-qualified top-2 deal (Zycus vs SAP Ariba) that has gone quiet on the buyer side. Three pre-award gates set on the 13 Aug 2025 call (architecture mapping, customer references, MSA legal pre-review) are still open, champion Zeeshan Pervez has been silent since the 30 Oct 2025 exco-delay note, and the only buyer touch since is a 24 Mar 2026 email declining an event invite. Rep outreach on 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 is unanswered. This is buyer deferral, not rep inactivity, and re-engaging the exco budget owner is the only real move.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-06. Close 2027-01-31 is 209 days out. Stage Vendor Selected reached but time-in-stage now exceeds ~13 months. Last substantive working call 13 Aug 2025 (~10.8 months ago); last two-way buyer touch an inbound email 24 Mar 2026 (~3.4 months ago); last Salesforce activity 15 Jun 2026 is rep-side and unanswered. Exco deferred at least once (30 Oct 2025). No credible path to the date without a fresh compelling event and re-activated executive advocacy.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025: 'Acen has confirmed that Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. (Other one is SAP-Ariba)' - Vendor Selected, two-horse race.

Avoma 13 Aug 2025 (Jeff De Sagun): three pre-award gates sequenced (architecture mapping then references then legal), targeted 'before the end of this month' - no completion evidence in the ~10.8 months since.
SF Next_Step__c 30 Oct 2025: 'Zee has advised management (exco) have decided to delay for 1 quarter' - last Zeeshan Pervez-specific signal, ~8 months ago.
SF activity 24 Mar 2026 (inbound email from Zeeshan Pervez): polite decline of an event invite - the only buyer-initiated touch since Oct 2025; rep outreach 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 is unanswered.
Best Case with three open pre-award gates and an ~8-month-silent champion is not defensible; recommend Pipeline.

metrics.narrative: Pain is well articulated but never quantified in money terms. On the 28 Feb 2025 call President Zeeshan Pervez stated ~70% of ACEN's spend is non-PO (loan, tax and other payments) creating a 'manual disconnect' from SAP, and the 6 May 2025 proposal-clarification call detailed the sizing (35 buyers, ~170 approvers, 300 requesters, 30,000 invoices over a 5-year term) but no cost, headcount or cycle-time savings figure was ever put on paper. ACEN's own selection weighting is 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability plus ROI (SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025), so a quantified value case is the exact lever the exco needs to justify spend against the significant SAP secondary-integration cost, and the wedge that neutralizes SAP Ariba's TCO edge. Building that ROI model with ACEN's finance lead is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zeeshan Pervez, ~70% non-PO spend)
Avoma call 6 May 2025 (user-count and invoice-volume commercials)
SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025 (50% functional / 50% tech+ROI weighting)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified but unengaged on a recorded call. Jonathan Back (CFO and Chief Strategy Officer) is the Salesforce Executive Sponsor and holds final budget; on 27 Feb 2025 Next_Step__c notes 'Zee, his boss (CFO Jonathan Back) are backing Zycus.' Zeeshan Pervez himself controls the approval path and said on 28 Feb 2025 'I'll approve it. My CFO is gonna approve it,' and 'there are 4 people in the company who's gonna make this decision.' The gap is not identity but activation: Jonathan Back has never appeared on a recorded call, and the project was explicitly de-scoped from the 2025 budget ('change of the procurement system is actually not part of our budget for the year 2025', Zeeshan Pervez 28 Feb 2025). Securing a CFO-level budget and sponsorship conversation is the mid-stage move that unblocks the exco decision.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Jonathan Back = Executive Sponsor)
SF Next_Step__c 27 Feb 2025
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zeeshan Pervez on approval authority and budget)

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and documented. On 7 Apr 2025 CIO-level contact Jennelyn Nicolas returned a selection-criteria table weighting the decision 50% functional fit and 50% technical capability plus ROI (SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025). Across the 6 May 2025 proposal-clarification call the buyer stress-tested functional fit in detail: user-role model (power vs business users), invoice volumes, supplier-portal cost, budget checking in Zycus vs SAP, and goods-receipt integration. Zeeshan Pervez added on 28 Feb 2025 that value and flexibility, not price alone, drive the choice ('one of the reasons why we'd make that decision is not necessarily always because of price... it's the added value'), and flagged data security as 'very heightened in our decision making'. Technical fit against the SAP ERP architecture is the sharpest criterion, addressed in the 13 Aug 2025 architecture-mapping session.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 7 Apr 2025 (Jennelyn Nicolas, 50/50 weighting)
Avoma call 6 May 2025 (functional clarifications)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zeeshan Pervez on value and security)

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal RFP-to-steering-committee flow that Zeeshan Pervez personally engineered. He described it on 28 Feb 2025: issue an RFP, keep evaluation to a few months, then a 4-person executive committee (including him and his CFO boss) takes the award decision. That played out - RFP invited 14 Mar 2025, onsite POC 11 Apr 2025, proposal and clarifications through May, down-select to top-2 on 3 Jun 2025. Reading the call sequence, the process then shifted to pre-award technical gating: the 13 Aug 2025 session was scoped by Jeff De Sagun as an end-to-end architecture walkthrough (supplier processes through the S2P layer down to the relevant stakeholder), with customer references and legal pre-review sequenced next before commercials. The blocker is the IT steering committee / exco, which deferred the whole initiative at least one quarter (30 Oct 2025) because the buy was never in the 2025 budget.
sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zeeshan Pervez, 4-person exco process)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (Jeff De Sagun, architecture then references then legal)
SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025 and 30 Oct 2025

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics were opened early but never advanced. On 13 Aug 2025 Madhuri proactively offered to send the Order Form and MSA for ACEN legal to pre-scan in parallel with references ('would you mind me sending you also a copy of order form? Perhaps you can pass it on to your legal... the legal really need a lot of time'), and Jeff De Sagun agreed. There is no evidence in the ~10.8 months since that legal review, redlines, MSA or SOW work actually began - the deal never reached commercials because the exco deferred it. As a Philippines/APAC new-business S2P deal it will need MSA plus Order Form plus a separately-signed SOW, and a PO is region-conditional. What would create visibility: confirm whether ACEN legal ever received or reviewed the Order Form/MSA, and whether the SAP secondary-integration scope belongs in the SOW.
sources: Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (Madhuri offers Order Form + MSA for legal pre-review; Jeff De Sagun agrees)
SF stage Vendor Selected (no contracting-stage progression logged)

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed, specific operational pain owned by procurement and finance. ACEN is replacing an in-house-built P2P system whose ~70% of spend (non-PO loan and tax payments) sits outside SAP with a 'manual disconnect' (Zeeshan Pervez, 28 Feb 2025), and he surfaced a bespoke internal 'RFP' (request-for-payment) module that no standard S2P vendor was asked to cover - a real fit risk if Zycus cannot absorb it. The strategic driver is institutionalizing a fast-growing global energy company ('setting up the company for the next 10 years') under ADB funding conditions that impose strict supplier code-of-conduct/ESG requirements, which is why Lythouse ESG surfaced (28 Feb 2025). Pain owners are Jeff De Sagun and Jennelyn Nicolas operationally and Zeeshan Pervez strategically. The counter-pressure is competing internal programs (HR-ERP go-live, a large billing-system revamp) that keep pushing the buy out.
sources: Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (Zeeshan Pervez, ~70% non-PO disconnect, RFP payment module, ADB/ESG)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (supplier-to-SAP architecture pain)
SF Products (replacing in-house P2P)

champion.narrative: Zeeshan Pervez (President ACEN Shared Services) was a strong, active champion through Feb-Oct 2025: he requested the Zycus RFP template to seed his own team without attribution ('Zee requested our RFP template which he can ask his team to use without telling it's coming from Zycus', SF Next_Step__c 6 Mar 2025 - clear insider advocacy), was 'backing Zycus' with his CFO boss (27 Feb 2025), and on the 28 Feb 2025 call personally committed to issuing the RFP and driving the exco. That advocacy has cooled: his last specific signal was the 30 Oct 2025 exco-delay note (~8 months ago), a 4 May 2026 lunch/Agentic-AI outreach and a 15 Jun 2026 Horizon invite are unanswered, and his only 2026 touch was declining an event on 24 Mar 2026. Jeff De Sagun (Senior IT Application Specialist, SF primary contact) is an engaged day-to-day operational driver but not an executive advocate. What developed: multi-thread depth across IT, procurement and finance and a top-2 position. What is missing: a re-activated executive champion pushing the steering/exco decision. This silence is the single biggest threat to the close.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 6 Mar 2025 (insider RFP-template request), 27 Feb 2025 ('backing Zycus'), 30 Oct 2025 (last Zee signal)
SF activity 24 Mar 2026 (Zeeshan Pervez declines event), 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 (rep outreach unanswered)
Avoma call 13 Aug 2025 (Jeff De Sagun driving operationally)

competition.narrative: Two-horse race down-selected to Zycus and SAP Ariba on 3 Jun 2025 (SF Next_Step__c: 'Zycus is shortlisted as top 2. Other one is SAP-Ariba'). SAP Ariba's structural edge is being SAP-native, which surfaces as ACEN's 'significant' SAP secondary integration/middleware cost concern (Zeeshan Pervez, 22 May 2025) - a genuine TCO disadvantage for Zycus on a SAP ERP shop. Zycus's wedge is functional and AI depth: Merlin AI contract risk-scoring and invoice OCR, non-SAP-locked configurable roles, a free unlimited supplier portal (6 May 2025), coverage of the bespoke non-PO payment module SAP Ariba was never scoped on, plus references (BDO, SM offered 13 Aug 2025) and a 12-18 month intake-management/conversational-AI lead Carl claimed on 28 Feb 2025. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua sit on Competitors__c but never recur in any call - earlier-round names now faded. The larger current threat than any vendor is do-nothing / repeated deferral driven by budget contention with ACEN's HR-ERP and billing programs.
sources: SF Next_Step__c 3 Jun 2025 (top-2 vs SAP Ariba)
SF Competitors__c (Coupa; GEP Worldwide; iValua; SAP Ariba)
SF Next_Step__c 22-29 May 2025 (SAP secondary cost)
Avoma call 28 Feb 2025 (in-house P2P incumbent)

deal_movement.summary: Reading Next_Step_History__c, field history and the last real buyer touch: the deal peaked Feb-Jun 2025 (down-select to top-2), stalled at the exco after 30 Oct 2025, and has only rep-side outreach in 2026. Ownership churned (Madhuri Govilkar -> Carl Kimball -> Laurice Quintero -> back to Carl Kimball on 28 Apr 2026) and the close date and amount were both revised down.

items.change: Down-selected to top-2 (Zycus vs SAP Ariba) - Vendor Selected
Amount revised down from 295k to 245k (likely scope reduction per 14 Oct note)
Exco deferred the procurement-digitization project by at least one quarter
Close date pushed from 15 May 2026 to 31 Jan 2027
Rep re-engagement attempt (lunch + Globe Agentic-AI bridge) via Zeeshan Pervez - unanswered
Rep Horizon Singapore event invite to Zeeshan Pervez - unanswered

competitive_position.summary: Live but frozen two-horse race: Zycus vs SAP Ariba, down-selected 3 Jun 2025. SAP Ariba is the single strongest current vendor threat because it is SAP-native and ACEN's 'significant' SAP secondary-integration cost (Zeeshan Pervez, 22 May 2025) hands it a TCO edge on a SAP ERP shop. The larger threat than any vendor is do-nothing / repeated exco deferral. The in-house-built P2P system is the incumbent being displaced. Coupa, GEP Worldwide and iValua sit on the Salesforce field but never recur in calls - faded earlier-round names.

competitors.quote: President Zee has confirmed that due to ongoing challenges with other initiatives, the Procurement Digitization project will be postponed by a quarter minimum.
competitors.how_we_win: Re-establish a compelling event and an exco-funded budget path; the bigger threat than any vendor is repeated deferral behind ACEN's HR-ERP and billing programs.

best_practice_check.summary: Mid-stage, top-2 deal stalled on buyer deferral. The win levers now are re-activating the executive champion and CFO, converting the SAP-cost objection into a quantified TCO story, and holding technical fit against SAP Ariba.
flags: Re-activate Zeeshan Pervez or route around him to CFO Jonathan Back: the champion has been silent ~8 months and two 2026 rep touches are unanswered, so a peer or value-led re-entry is the highest-impact lever now.

Convert the 'significant SAP secondary-integration cost' objection into a quantified TCO/ROI model with ACEN finance - this is both the exco's missing justification and the wedge that neutralizes SAP Ariba's SAP-native edge.
Confirm whether the initiative is funded for 2026: it was explicitly never in the 2025 budget and exco deferred it, so a compelling event and budget path matter more than any product step.

Champion is at risk and is the deal's root stall.

Carl positioned the Gen-AI intake-management edge on 28 Feb 2025 and Merlin AI risk-scoring/OCR on 6 May 2025, but AI never became a decision driver.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 4 / AIS_Status Negative reflects an S2P suite with no AI SKUs and a SAP-integration focus. Calls corroborate: buyer interest centred on functional fit, SAP cost and security, not AI. The one AI thread is a 4 May 2026 rep idea to bridge ACEN to Globe Telecom on Agentic AI - a future hook, not current appetite.

scope_change.detail: On 14 Oct 2025 Zeeshan Pervez indicated closing within a budget he could get through exco, with Carl to 'revise numbers... likely reduce scope'; amount was cut from 295k to 245k on 27 Oct 2025. A defensive, cost-driven narrowing amid competing internal programs.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus is one of the final two (vs SAP Ariba) on a well-qualified ACEN S2P deal, but the win has frozen: executive champion Zeeshan Pervez has been silent ~8 months, the CFO budget owner has never been on a call, and the project was deferred off the 2025 budget - re-activating an exco-funded decision is the only thing between us and a slow no-decision.

win_position.text: We hold a top-2 position vs SAP Ariba (down-select 3 Jun 2025) with a POC-validated 50/50 functional-plus-tech fit, but score is capped below the Vendor-Selected ceiling because the deal has no active champion and no engaged economic buyer right now.
SAP Ariba's SAP-native edge is live: Zeeshan Pervez flagged a 'significant' SAP secondary-integration cost on 22 May 2025 that we have not yet countered with a quantified TCO model.
Champion Zeeshan Pervez has been silent since the 30 Oct 2025 exco-delay note and unresponsive to 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 outreach, so our advocacy inside the steering committee is currently zero.

deal_momentum.text: No buyer-facing working session since the 13 Aug 2025 architecture call and three pre-award gates (references, architecture sign-off, legal pre-review) remain open ~10.8 months later.
The close date has slipped twice (to 31 Jan 2027) and the exco deferred the whole initiative by at least a quarter on 30 Oct 2025.
The only 2026 activity is unanswered rep outreach; the last buyer-initiated touch was a 24 Mar 2026 email declining an event.

customer_commitment.text: ACEN ran a full formal evaluation - RFP, onsite POC (11 Apr 2025), proposal clarifications and a top-2 down-select - and Zeeshan Pervez personally requested our RFP template to seed his team (6 Mar 2025), real prior commitment.
Commitment has since decayed: the buy was explicitly never in the 2025 budget, scope was cut from 295k to 245k, and no exco decision has been reconvened.

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is no-decision / repeated deferral behind ACEN's HR-ERP and billing programs, compounded by an unconfirmed 2026 budget.
Executive-alignment risk: champion cooling and CFO Jonathan Back never engaged directly leave no one driving the award internally.

deal_momentum.evidence: No buyer call since 13 Aug 2025; two close-date slips; exco deferral 30 Oct 2025; only unanswered rep outreach in 2026.

customer_commitment.evidence: Full evaluation and top-2 down-select vs off-budget deferral and a scope cut.

deal_risk.evidence: No-decision risk plus cooling champion and unengaged EB.

customer_preference.evidence: Historically strong lean (top-2 vs SAP Ariba; Zeeshan Pervez requested our RFP template to seed his team and said value/flexibility not price would decide, 28 Feb 2025), but decaying with ~8 months of buyer silence and no fresh Zycus-specific pull.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain is clear (~70% non-PO 'manual disconnect', ADB/ESG supplier compliance) but no ROI, savings or cycle-time figure was ever quantified; ACEN's own weighting demands a 50% tech-plus-ROI case.

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — On 14 Oct 2025 Zeeshan Pervez indicated closing within a budget he could get through exco, with Carl to 'revise numbers... likely reduce scope'; amount was cut from 295k to 245k on 27 Oct 2025. A defe. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.
reasons.detail: On 14 Oct 2025 Zeeshan Pervez indicated closing within a budget he could get through exco, with Carl to 'revise numbers... likely reduce scope'; amount was cut from 295k to 245k on 27 Oct 2025. A defensive, cost-driven narrowing amid competing internal programs.

items.action: Send Zeeshan Pervez a value-led re-entry note tied to a quantified SAP-cost/ROI angle.
items.trigger: Champion silent since 30 Oct 2025; 4 May and 15 Jun 2026 rep touches unanswered.
items.expected_effect: Reopens the executive channel that gates the exco decision; if no reply in 10 business days, escalate to an exec-to-exec touch.

items.action: Build a quantified TCO and ROI model countering the SAP secondary-integration cost with ACEN finance.
items.trigger: Zeeshan Pervez flagged significant SAP secondary-integration cost as a blocker.
items.expected_effect: Gives the exco its missing budget justification and neutralizes SAP Ariba's SAP-native TCO edge.

items.action: Route to CFO Jonathan Back for a budget-sponsorship conversation via the champion or CEO Eric Francia.
items.trigger: EB verbally backed Zycus but has never been on a recorded call; project off the 2025 budget.
items.expected_effect: Engages the real budget owner to confirm 2026 funding and unstall the award.

items.action: Deliver curated energy-industry and complex-account references (BDO, SM) to Jeff De Sagun.
items.trigger: References were offered on 13 Aug 2025 and never delivered - an open pre-award gate.
items.expected_effect: Closes one of the three open pre-award gates and rebuilds working momentum.

contributions.evidence: scope narrowed vs prior — On 14 Oct 2025 Zeeshan Pervez indicated closing within a budget he could get through exco, with Carl to 'revise numbers... likely reduce scope'; amount was cut  (buyer likely defensive on cost or phased implementation)
Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.win_position: Win 53 is carried by preference strength +0.40 (weight 20); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 60.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 29 is anchored on the stage win (53), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'SAP ERP')
